Arundo donax, commonly known as giant cane or arundo, is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, providing various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Arundo donax include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Arundo donax is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions.
